FQ 6101A.description: The rigid body equations of motion for an aircraft are derived, and then trim solutions are presented. The equations are linearized in both state space as well as transfer function methods.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 6201A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 6202E and FQ 6203Z under a shared description block.
FQ 6201A.description: The concepts of static and dynamic stability are applied to aircraft. Aircraft modes of motion are introduced. Stability derivatives and their influence are presented. Finally, MIL-STD 1797B guidelines are introduced and applied to the static and dynamic stability parameters to predict an aircraft's handling qualities or to assess a specification compliance. Concepts taught in this course are reinforced using a simulation laboratory exercise.

Finally, the event will introduce CR operations to CSO/P/RPA aircrew.

FQ 6301A.eventName: T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Brief

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 flying qualities test plan brief

FQ 6301A.description: This event introduces the students to the flying qualities T-38 test plan. It will introduce the plan in three parts. The first part is longitudinal where students are left with constructing cards from given test objectives and measures of performance. The second part is lateral-directional where students will develop measures of performance and cards from given objectives. The third part will be conducted later after handling qualities academics. In that phase students will have to determine objectives, measures of performance and develop cards from a set of requirements. In all parts, students will be responsible for defending their test approaches and mitigation/build-up plans to a reviewing T38 IP and IFTE.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 flying qualities test plan data flight (series position 1)

FQ 6310F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'FQ 6310-1F T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Data Flight 1-2'.
FQ 6310F.description: These are student designed and planned flights allowing the students to gather data in support of their flying qualities test plan report.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Dynamics Control Room (ABM/FTE with CSO/P/RPA desired as DE)
T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Working Group

FQ 6311F.eventName: T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Data Flight 2

FQ 6311F.description: These are student designed and planned flights allowing the students to gather data in support of their flying qualities test plan report.

T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Working Group

FQ 6320W.eventName: T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Working Group

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 FQ test plan working group

FQ 6320W.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 6321R under a shared description block. Event type suffix 'W' (Working Group) not in base knowledge-base suffix table; treated as its own type.
FQ 6320W.description: This a project composes several events, all tightly integrated to guide the student along its progression. The student will explore MIL-STD-1797 while collecting data in an unsupervised fashion. It will give the student the opportunity to participate in planning a test across multiple flights while ensuring the quality of the data collected is high enough to write a report detailing the findings. The student will develop measures of performance and test maneuvers to assess the open loop flying qualities of the T-38 as an area defense fighter. Not only does this event illustrate how flying qualities or spec-compliance testing might happen, but it also enforces requirements traceability and associates test maneuvers with test objectives via measures of performance. All the student's testing experience to this point will be required to make a final recommendation as to whether to purchase this aircraft for a developing nation.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Brief
T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Data Flight 2 [req'd for FQ 6321R]

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 7101A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 7102E and FQ 7103Z under a shared description block.
FQ 7101A.description: Feedback flight control systems are introduced along with motivation for transition from mechanical to automatic flight control systems. Linear analysis techniques are presented with applications to aircraft flight controls. These fundamental concepts will be used throughout the 7000-level course. Feedback flight control system implementation is introduced to demonstrate how augmented control systems are used to enhance air vehicle stability and performance. Examples of classical proportional, integral, derivative and LQR (linear quadratic regulator) control systems are presented as is gain scheduling, mode shaping. Basic linear control design and analysis techniques are then applied to aircraft. Considerations for modern aircraft with relevant examples are given as well as issues associated with flight testing control systems. Finally, an introduction to digital control and other analysis planes are discussed.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 7201A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 7202A, FQ 7203E, and FQ 7204Z under a shared description block.
FQ 7201A.description: Static structural analysis is presented along with basic stress/strain analysis. Dynamics applied to non-rigid bodies is also presented, to include basic dynamics and ending with flutter and aeroservoelasticity. A fundamental exposition of basic analysis techniques used for aircraft structures is reviewed. Statics and dynamic structural test. Techniques are presented and include both ground and flight test considerations and buildup.

FQ 7301A.eventName: Data-Driven Analysis and Control for Domain Sciences Lecture

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 7301A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 7302E and FQ 7303Z under a shared description block.
FQ 7301A.description: This block will cover advanced Data-Driven control techniques. It includes a broad scope of topics that build an understanding of aspects to consider when testing an autonomy-based system.  Topics include identifying and analyzing different types of control systems and when to apply them, describing different categories of data and proper methods for their analysis, describing different forms and fidelity of simulation and their application, analyzing the use of Monte Carlo testing to include its advantages and pitfalls, and identifying key issues to consider when building up to and executing flight tests. It is taught consecutively with AS 6600 Data-Driven Control.

FQ 8210Y.eventName: T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Oral Report

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 handling qualities test plan oral report

FQ 8210Y.description: This project is a continuation of the FQ 6301A Flying Qualities Test Plan Project. Building on the knowledge the student gained from that project, they will first design handling qualities tasks to evaluate the mission performance of the T-38 as an area defense fighter for foreign military sales. The student will be briefed in the FQ 8201M T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan MIB where expectations will be set and requirements for the project will be discussed. Next they will design tasks and present test cards to be signed by a T-38 Instructor Pilot. The student will then go execute their plan to gather data in FQ 8202F T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Data Flight. Once you have collected and analyzed all your data, you will present your findings to a board in the FQ 8210Y T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Oral Report.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Flying Qualities Test Plan Oral Report
T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Data Flight

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9101A.description: Various vehicle failure modes are evaluated in this block. Specifically, engine out testing is addressed. Failure mode and effect testing/analysis are addressed.

FQ 9201A.eventName: Stall, Departure and Spin Testing Lecture

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9201A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9202E and FQ 9203Z under a shared description block.
FQ 9201A.description: The aerodynamics associated with stall are presented and various high-angle-of-attack terms are formally defined. The equations of motion are revisited for coupling and spins and then analyzed. High angle-of- attack test methodologies are presented including those discussed by MIL-STD-1797B and MIL-F-83691B. Analysis and recovery techniques for this regime of flight are developed for use in future flight tests.

FQ 9202E.eventName: Stall, Departure and Spin Testing Exam

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

Analysis and recovery techniques for this regime of flight are developed for use in future flight tests.

FQ 9230M.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9231F under a shared description block.
FQ 9230M.description: The objective of this mission is to train students in the proper accomplishment of F-16 nose-high slow-speed recoveries, recognition of departure from controlled flight, post-departure recovery procedures and pitch-rocking techniques, and demonstrate potential errors and their consequences.

FQ 9240M.eventName: F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion MIB

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — F-16 high AOA envelope expansion MIB

FQ 9240M.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9241W, FQ 9242W, FQ 9243W, FQ 9244S, FQ 9245C, FQ 9246F, FQ 9247C, and FQ 9248Y under a shared description block. MCG prereq list includes alternative prereqs joined by 'or' (FQ 6252C or TF 6231C); both captured.
FQ 9240M.description: For the High AOA Envelope Expansion project, the student will receive project requirements and be given information needed to complete the project at the MIB. The students will then be given time to develop a plan during the instructor facilitated FQ 9241W F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion TPWG 1. The students will then be broken up into teams where they will finalize their plan during FQ 9242W F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion TPWG 2 and FQ 9243W High AOA Envelope Expansion Flight Readiness Review. The students will demonstrate to their instructors that they can execute their plan in the rehearsal sim and control room. Finally, the teams will execute their plan on the data flight and control room. The team will analyze and report on their data as part of FQ 9248Y F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion Oral Report.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Dynamics Control Room (ABM/FTE with CSO/P/RPA as DE) [req'd for FQ 9245C]

group.description: MCG lists FQ 6252C and TF 6231C as 'or' alternatives. Either control-room intro satisfies the prereq for FQ 9245C.

FQ 9247C.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9240M, FQ 9241W, FQ 9242W, FQ 9243W, FQ 9244S, FQ 9245C, FQ 9246F, and FQ 9248Y under a shared description block. Primary applicability ABM/FTE; CSO/P/RPA 'desired not required as DE' is a supporting-role descriptor. DE = Disciplined Engineer (control-room role per MCG p.87 prose). CSO/P/RPA students may fill the DE role in the CR; primary applicability remains ABM/FTE.
FQ 9247C.description: For the High AOA Envelope Expansion project, the student will receive project requirements and be given information needed to complete the project at the MIB. The students will then be given time to develop a plan during the instructor facilitated FQ 9241W F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion TPWG 1. The students will then be broken up into teams where they will finalize their plan during FQ 9242W F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion TPWG 2 and FQ 9243W High AOA Envelope Expansion Flight Readiness Review. The students will demonstrate to their instructors that they can execute their plan in the rehearsal sim and control room. Finally, the teams will execute their plan on the data flight and control room. The team will analyze and report on their data as part of FQ 9248Y F-16 High AOA Envelope Expansion Oral Report.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Dynamics Control Room (ABM/FTE with CSO/P/RPA as DE) [req'd for FQ 9245C]

group.description: MCG lists FQ 6252C and TF 6231C as 'or' alternatives. Either control-room intro satisfies the prereq for FQ 9245C.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9301A.description: The analytical process and flight test methods used to qualify stores on aircraft for safe operation are introduced.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9401A.description: This block introduces the concept of aircraft flight envelope expansion as well as the creation of aircraft flight manuals using testing that will occur during the Flying Qualities Phase.

FQ 9410A.eventName: First Flight and Envelope Expansion Lecture

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9410A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9411E and FQ 9412Z under a shared description block.
FQ 9410A.description: The process leading to first flight of an aircraft is presented. Issues associated with experimental flight test are presented.

FQ 9411E.eventName: First Flight and Envelope Expansion Exam

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9411E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9410A and FQ 9412Z under a shared description block.
FQ 9411E.description: The process leading to first flight of an aircraft is presented. Issues associated with experimental flight test are presented.

FQ 9412Z.eventName: First Flight and Envelope Expansion Debrief

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

FQ 9412Z.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9410A and FQ 9411E under a shared description block.
FQ 9412Z.description: The process leading to first flight of an aircraft is presented. Issues associated with experimental flight test are presented.

FQ 9501M.eventName: Sim Flying Qualities Practical Exam MIB (ABM/CSO/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM/CSO/RPA — sim flying qualities practical exam MIB (goal-event series MIB)

FQ 9501M.eventNotes: Introductory MIB for FQ 9502S goal-event series. MCG lists FQ 9501M on its own line above the grouped FQ 9502S / FQ 9503R description block; no explicit prereqs listed for FQ 9501M.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM/CSO/RPA goal event — sim flying qualities practical exam

FQ 9502S.eventNotes: Goal event for ABM/CSO/RPA. Grouped in MCG with FQ 9503R under a shared description block.
FQ 9502S.description: The objective of the sim is to evaluate the student ABM/CSO/RPA ability to plan, execute, and report a flying and handling qualities evaluation using hand-held data techniques. The students are given a scenario in which they must conduct an initial evaluation of a proposed solution to address the significant time delays often encountered when flying RPAs or handling remotely guided munitions. The student is tasked to plan and fly the sim then provide an informal written report on the results and recommendations.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Oral Report

FQ 9503R.eventName: Sim Flying Qualities Practical Exam Written Report (ABM/CSO/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM/CSO/RPA — sim flying qualities practical exam written report

FQ 9503R.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with FQ 9502S under a shared description block.
FQ 9503R.description: The objective of the sim is to evaluate the student ABM/CSO/RPA ability to plan, execute, and report a flying and handling qualities evaluation using hand-held data techniques. prerequisites.name: T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Oral Report

FQ 9510M.eventName: T-38 Flying Qualities Practical Exam MIB (FTE/P)

applicabilityDetail.description: FTE/P — T-38 flying qualities practical exam MIB

FQ 9510M.eventNotes: Introductory MIB for FQ 9511F / FQ 9512C goal-event pair. Grouped in MCG with FQ 9511F, FQ 9512C, and FQ 9513R under a shared description block.
FQ 9510M.description: The objective of the sortie is to evaluate the student pilot/FTE ability to plan, execute, and report a flying and handling qualities evaluation. The students are given a scenario in which information on the T-38 pitch PIO susceptibility at high speed and low altitude is requested. It will take more than one sortie to provide this information, so an engineering team elects to fly a single sortie to narrow the focus of the effort. The students are tasked to plan and fly this sortie then provide an informal written report to the engineering team on the results and recommendations for worthwhile additional testing.

prerequisites.name: T-38 Handling Qualities Test Plan Oral Report

applicabilityDetail.description: P goal event — T-38 flying qualities practical exam (flight side of paired practical)
